Center: IAEA Nuclear Data Section, Vienna, Austria
ref: Jour: J.Nuclear Energy A&B (Reactor Sci. and Technol.), Vol.18, p.417 (1964)
title: Fission spectrum averaged cross sections of threshold reactions

x4freetext: Fission spectrum averaged cross sections of threshold
reactions

x4codes.hlp: Australian Nucl.Sci.and Techn.Org., Lucas Heights,NSW, Australia

x4codes.shortRef: Jour: J.Nuclear Energy A&B (Reactor Sci. and Technol.), Vol.18, p.417 (1964)

x4codes.hlp: Australian Nucl.Sci.and Techn.Org., Lucas Heights,NSW, Australia

x4freetext: MOATA reactor
Moata reactor of UTR10 type with neutron
converter, see `INC-SPEC`.
.Samples were irradiated at center of a neutron
converter consisting of an air-cooled hollow cylinder
of enriched U-Al alloy,2.5 cm dia.,10 cm long,
containing 10 gram of U-235.
Spectrum at the center of neutron converter conformed
closely to the U-235 fission spectrum. Fast flux was
4.14 X 10**10 n/cm2/sec. Daily variation was monitored
27Al(n,alpha)24Na reaction. Fast flux from fuel
elements was 10**9 n/cm2/sec, less than 2.5% of fast
flux inside the converter.
Spectrographically pure metals or oxides.
The absolute activity of the samples were
measured using NaI(Tl) crystal spectrometers of
various sizes (1 X 14 inches, 8 X 4 inches; and
2 X 2 inches crystals in 4pi geometry)
4pi beta- proportional counter was used for
counting the separated beta-emitting products of
31P(n,p)31Si and 32S(n,p)32P

x4freetext: Separated isotope
Uncertainty in branching ratios has not been included
in error given. Assumed values of decay parameters are
given in J.Nucl.Energy A/B 18,417(1964)
** Author questions isotopic purity because results
given here disagree with results obtained with sample
of natural composition using higher flux HIFAR
reactor.  See Exfor 30144.003. **

x4freetext: The partial cross section was determined from the
build up of Co-58 gamma activity in nickel samples.
No corrections were necessary for thermal burn up of
either Co-58 or Co-58m because irradiation times were
short.
